title: The Century of the Self
description: How Freud's ideas were weaponised to sell products and control populations — a 4-part BBC series that changes how you see advertising forever.

title: The Internet's Own Boy
description: The story of Aaron Swartz — prodigy, activist, and co-creator of RSS and Reddit — and the legal battle that ended his life. Essential internet history.

title: HyperNormalisation
description: A BBC documentary about how politicians, bankers, and technologists created a simplified version of the world — and why reality became impossible to navigate.

title: Citizenfour
description: The real-time footage of Edward Snowden revealing NSA mass surveillance — filmed as it happened in a Hong Kong hotel room. Won the Oscar for Best Documentary.

title: The Corporation
description: If a corporation were a person, what kind of person would it be? Psychologists apply the DSM to the behaviour of modern corporations. Uncomfortable answer.

title: Cosmos: Possible Worlds
description: Neil deGrasse Tyson takes you from the Big Bang to the far future — making the universe feel personal and awe-inspiring at the same time. Free on YouTube.

title: Free Solo
description: Alex Honnold climbs El Capitan with no ropes. 3,000 feet. No safety equipment. The most terrifying and beautiful thing ever filmed. Oscar winner.

title: 13th
description: The 13th Amendment abolished slavery — except as punishment for crime. This documentary traces how that loophole shaped mass incarceration in America.

title: My Octopus Teacher
description: A filmmaker befriends an octopus in a South African kelp forest over one year. One of the most moving wildlife documentaries ever made. Netflix / YouTube.

title: Abstract: The Art of Design
description: Episodes profiling legendary designers — Tinker Hatfield (Nike), Ilse Crawford, Paula Scher — showing how design shapes every experience of your life.
